topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Maya was scrolling through her feed, a dull afternoon spent catching up on campus gossip, when a notification popped up. It was from Professor Albright. Her heart gave a sudden, sharp lurch. She clicked it, expecting a syllabus update or perhaps a reminder about office hours. Instead, there was a picture of him, smiling warmly, overlaid with a caption that read, "Great discussion today, Maya! Keep up the insightful questions. 😉." Maya froze, her thumb hovering over the screen. She blinked several times, rereading the message, then looked up at the professor's empty desk in the lecture hall, a strange heat rising in her cheeks. Who on earth was this person, and why was he sharing her interactions?

[story 2]

Liam had been meticulously organizing his digital photo albums, a calming ritual after a stressful week of exams. He stumbled across an old folder labeled "History Class - Fall 2023." Inside, buried beneath lecture slides, was a series of posts. They were photos from class—a candid shot of him helping a struggling student with a complex concept, captioned with a thoughtful analysis. Then, he saw the most recent one: a picture of Liam, looking slightly less formal, captioned simply, "Thinking about the next chapter. Always appreciate the engagement in this course." Liam dropped the phone onto his bed. He picked it up again, his breath catching in his throat. He stared at the screen, his jaw slack, unable to form a coherent thought. The air in his room suddenly felt thin.

[story 3]

From the moment Sarah saw the profile picture—the familiar, distinguished face of her literature instructor—she felt a profound disorientation. It was late; she was supposed to be studying for her final. A notification chimed, and she navigated to the profile. There, a recent story showed the professor reviewing student essays, and the accompanying text was a direct reply to a comment Sarah had made on a previous assignment. Sarah leaned back in her chair, the cheap plastic groaning under her weight. She slowly brought her hand up to cover her mouth, her eyes wide and unblinking. She let out a small, involuntary sound—a mixture of disbelief and sheer bewilderment—before forcing herself to type a question into the direct message box.
